one_line_takeaway: LLMs internally encode contextual-privacy norms as three independent CI-aligned directions but fail to act on them; steering along each CI parameter separately (not a single privacy vector) reliably suppresses leakage and transfers across benchmarks.
key_finding: Concept-level probes achieve near-perfect internal classification of privacy norms (AUROC rising from ~0.53 in early layers to >0.90 in deep layers), yet Llama-3.1-8B still discloses private information in 42.5% of 200 behavioral scenarios (NCR 57.5%), and all four models leak in up to 39% of CONFAIDE Tier 3 scenarios — the "privacy awareness gap." The signal is multi-dimensional: a single PCA direction gets only 0.43 AUROC on CONFAIDE Tier 2, but PCA-3 reaches 0.77 and a transferred probe reaches 0.84. The subspace selectivity test shows each CI parameter's subspace classifies its own five categories at ~100% but drops to ~19-27% on the other parameters, confirming independence. CI-parametric steering reduces Llama-3.1 synthetic leakage from 42.5% to 0.5% (PPI 98.8%; note Table 1 prints 5.0% and the abstract says 5%, but the reported PPI=98.8% and Appendix Table 4 both imply 0.5%). It transfers robustly: Llama-3.1 CONFAIDE 24.1%->0.0% and PrivaCI-Bench 18.0%->0.0% at alpha=1.0, whereas monolithic additive steering can worsen leakage (e.g., Llama-3.1 CONFAIDE 24.1%->47.4% at alpha=0.5; PrivaCI-Bench up to 64.7%). Steering along any single CI axis in isolation increases leakage, but combining all three cuts it by ~89% on CONFAIDE.
core_contribution: The paper (Wang, Xiong, Shu, Emory) presents what it claims is the first systematic study of contextual privacy in LLMs as a structured latent representation grounded in Contextual Integrity (CI) theory. Its own two contributions are: (1) a probing analysis showing that the three norm-determining CI parameters (information type, recipient, transmission principle) are encoded as linearly separable, functionally independent directions in the residual stream, yet models still leak in behavior — a "privacy awareness gap"; and (2) CI-parametric steering, a novel inference-time method that steers independently along each per-parameter CI direction (h'_l = h_l + alpha * sum_p v_l^(p)) rather than along a single monolithic privacy direction. Note: prior work it CITES but does NOT claim as its own includes representation engineering / LAT (Zou et al. 2023), ITI (Li et al. 2023), activation engineering (Turner et al. 2023), the SALT chain-of-thought steering method (Batra et al. 2025), and the CI benchmarks CONFAIDE (Mireshghallah et al. 2024) and PrivaCI-Bench (Li et al. 2025a), which it uses for evaluation but did not build.
threat_model: The concern is contextual privacy leakage: an LLM deployed in high-stakes interactive roles (healthcare, personal assistants, social role-play) that inappropriately discloses private information about a data subject to an unauthorized recipient, violating context-specific norms even though no adversarial attacker is required — the model volunteers protected information when asked in-character. Framed via Contextual Integrity, a violation is an information flow whose sender/recipient/information-type/transmission-principle breaches established norms. CI-parametric steering is the defense (inference-time activation intervention). The paper notes it does not yet evaluate against adaptive adversarial or simulation-based attacks that iteratively probe the defense.
limitations: Behavioral-gap and steering experiments center on Llama-3.1-8B; transfer results cover four 7-8B models but no larger/proprietary models. Evaluation relies entirely on a GPT-4o-mini judge for leak/refuse/appropriate labels, so metrics inherit judge bias. There is an internal inconsistency: the abstract and Table 1 state 5% synthetic leakage while the reported PPI (98.8%) and Appendix Table 4 indicate 0.5%. The main method deliberately drops two of five CI parameters (data subject, sender); including them causes catastrophic failure, showing the approach is sensitive to which axes are chosen. Most importantly, steering three axes at once carries a real utility cost — Alpaca helpfulness/coherence/relevance collapse toward 1.0 at alpha=1.0 — so low leakage can partly reflect degraded output quality; the authors argue lower alpha and per-parameter alpha tuning mitigate this but do not fully resolve it. Transfer is imperfect for some models (e.g., Llama-2 CONFAIDE leakage rises to 43.3% at alpha=1.0). No adaptive/adversarial evaluation.

datasets_used: Synthetic CI-grounded probe data (500 concept-level matched pairs, 200 balanced behavioral role-play scenarios, 1,500 CI-parametric examples)
CONFAIDE Tier 2 and Tier 3 (Mireshghallah et al. 2024; Tier 3 = 270 multi-party confidentiality scenarios)
PrivaCI-Bench (Li et al. 2025a; GDPR/HIPAA regulatory scenarios)
quantitative_results: Llama-3.1-8B leaks 42.5% on 200 synthetic behavioral scenarios (NCR 57.5%); leaks 24.1% on CONFAIDE Tier 3
Concept-level AUROC rises ~0.53 (early layers) to >0.90 (deep layers); single PCA on CONFAIDE Tier 2 = 0.43, PCA-3 = 0.77, transferred probe up to 0.84
CI-parametric steering: synthetic leakage 42.5%->0.5%, PPI 98.8% (Table 1 prints 5.0%; PPI and Appendix Table 4 imply 0.5%) vs Additive 21.5%, LoRRA 16.0%, Rep Tuning 37.0%
Zero-shot transfer at alpha=1.0: CONFAIDE Tier 3 Llama-3.1 24.1%->0.0%, Mistral 25.9%->1.9%, Qwen 38.5%->15.2%, Llama-2 23.7%->43.3%; PrivaCI-Bench Llama-3.1 18.0%->0.0%, Mistral 10.7%->0.7%
Monolithic additive steering worsens leakage: CONFAIDE Llama-3.1 24.1%->47.4% (alpha=0.5), PrivaCI-Bench Llama-3.1 18.0%->64.7% (alpha=1.0)
Subspace selectivity: diagonal ~100%, off-diagonal ~19-27%; permutation significance 6.1 sigma (Llama-3.1/Qwen), 3.7 sigma (Llama-2), 2.1 sigma (Mistral)
CI-parameter ablation on CONFAIDE: single-axis steering raises leakage to 58.5% (info type), 62.6% (recipient), 53.3% (transmission); all three combined 2.6% (PPI 89.2%)
Five-parameter variant dilutes signal: synthetic PPI drops 98.8%->62.4%; Llama-2 catastrophic (leakage 52%->88%)
Utility cost: CI-parametric on Alpaca degrades helpfulness/coherence/relevance to ~1.0/1.0/1.0 at alpha=1.0 vs 4.58/4.76/4.89 unsteered
